title: Azorella columnifer
taxonomy: Azorella columnifer is classified as a species within the genus Azorella, which belongs to the family Apiaceae. This classification places it in the order Apiales, and further categorizes it as a member of the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is part of the clade Angiosperms, which consists of flowering plants. The clade Angiosperms is a subgroup of the larger clade Spermatophyta, which encompasses all vascular plants with seeds. This comprehensive classification highlights the plant's evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Azorella columnae (also known as Azorella columnifer) is a perennial herb native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes mountains. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, gastrointestinal problems, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Azorella columnae include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditional herbal preparations made from Azorella columnae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and digestive issues.
